name: Tempio Grande at Vulci
description: A major urban sanctuary at Vulci with a long period of use, stretching from the archaic period into the Roman period.

name: Fortuna
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 D3 Fortuna

name: Jouima
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 28 C2 Jouima

name: Agueneb
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 G3 Agueneb

name: Djedars
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 G1 Djedars

name: Ste-Barbe-du-Tlélat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 E1 Ste-Barbe-du-Tlélat

name: Chettaba
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 E4 Chettaba

name: Unnamed Gallo-Roman temple
description: The site of an unnamed Gallo-Roman temple is situated in Thésée, Arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay, Centre-Val de Loire, France. This temple site is located close to the ruins at Bâtiments des potiers de Pouillé.

name: Khwaja Ali Sehyaka/Sehyak
description: This "Mesopotamian-style temple" site located near modern Dishu in Afghanistan's Helmand province was excavated in the 1970s. Carbon-14 dates and "Greek-style architectural fragments and fine red pottery" led the excavators to date the use of the site from the late Hellenistic and Parthian period to "the beginning of the Sasanian period in the 3rd century" CE. Finds included a fragmentary bilingual (Greek/Aramaic) inscription possibly mentioning a Parthian king Phraates and the Iranian deity Ahuramazda.

name: Ursins Gallo-Roman temple
description: Site of a Gallo-Roman temple.

name: Sun Temple of Niuserre
description: Temple dedicated to the god Ra in the 5th dynasty by the pharaoh Niuserre (Nyuserre).

name: Temple of Enodia
description: Pherai was the main cult center of the goddess Enodia. Today, the foundations of the temple of Enodia (or Ennodia) at found at the site.

name: Temple of Veiovis
description: The Temple of Veiovis, also attested as Aedes Vediovis, was a temple of ancient Rome located on the Capitoline Hill in a location known as "inter duos lucos" that is interpreted to mean a saddle between the two summits of the hill. The remains of a temple podium were discovered beneath the northwest corner of the Palazzo Senatore in 1938-1939.

name: Lupercal
description: A cave located at the foot of the Palatine Hill in Rome in which the she-wolf was said to have been found suckling the twins, Romulus and Remus.

name: Northern sanctuary at Kythnos
description: Religious structure in Kythnos constructed in the Archaic period.

name: Esterzili
description: The Domu de Orgia at Esterzili is the largest nuragic "megaron" structure thus far discovered in Sardinia.

name: So-called Oratory of Phalaris
description: A small, rectangular Greek temple-type building dated to the first century B.C. and located in the upper agora of Agrigento, on the Poggetto di San Nicola. The name "Oratory" comes from its later use as a Christian chapel.

props.ancientName: Oratory of Phalaris, Oratory of Phalarys

name: Windmill Hill (Athens)
description: A now-disappeared terrace wall and rock cuttings were thought to be the remains of an ancient sanctuary of uncertain identity.

name: Mithraeum at Vindovala
description: A temple sacred to Mithras located at the Roman fort of Vindovala (Rudchester) on Hadrian's Wall.

name: Temple of Claudius
description: A large classical-style Roman temple built shortly after the conquest of Britannia in A.D. 43 by the emperor Claudius. Its remains lie beneath Colchester Castle.

name: Large Temple at Sikait
description: A large rock-cut temple at Sikait in Egypt's Eastern desert.

name: Temple of Castor and Pollux, Cora
description: The Temple of Castor and Pollux at Cora was originally built between the fourth and the second centuries B.C. The partially extant architrave carries an inscription of the first century B.C.

name: Gremoulias ancient sanctuary
description: A sanctuary -- with remains of at least two successive temples (6th C BCE and 4th C BCE), a great altar, and votive deposits -- located on the modern Gremoulias saddle, 3.5km SE of Kalavryta in the northern Peloponnese. The site is not known from ancient literature. Scholars speculate that the deity worshiped may have been Artemis and the site may have been an extra-urban sanctuary of nearby Kynaitha.

name: Allègre
description: The site of sulphur springs that also revealed evidence for a sanctuary that included 24 altars, so of which carried dedications either to nymphs or mother goddesses. A number of Roman artifacts indicate activity at the site from the Flavian to the Antonine period.

name: Château-Bas (Roman Temple)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 Château-Bas

name: Châtelard de Lardiers
description: A Celto-Ligurian oppidum and sanctuary.

name: Coste Guizon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A1 Coste Guizon

name: Lachau
description: A Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: Notre-Dame-de-Laval
description: A pre-Roman and Gallo-Roman sanctuary at the locality called l'Ermitage de Notre-Dame-de-Laval.

name: Octon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A2 Octon

name: Roquepertuse
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 Roquepertuse

name: Roujan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A2 Roujan

name: L’Acapte
description: Site of a rural sanctuary dedicated to Aristaeus, son of Apollo.

name: Fox-Amphoux
description: At Fox-Amphoux the remains of a Roman villa were discovered, adjacent to those of a sanctuary ad fines that may have been dedicated to the Capitoline triad. The site was in use from the time of Augustus until the third century AD.

name: Temple of Zeus Olympius (Jerash)
description: The monumental sanctuary of Zeus Olympius at Jerash dates to the 160s CE.

name: Asea Vigla
description: A Doric temple dated to the sixth century BCE.

name: Temple of Venus Genetrix
description: The Temple of Venus Genetrix located in the Forum Iulium was dedicated ca. 46 BCE.

props.ancientName: Tempio di Venere Genitrice al Foro di Cesare

name: Temple of Talamonaccio
description: An Etruscan temple of the fourth century BCE, associated with the ancient settlement of Telamon. It lies on Mount Talamonaccio, across the bay from the modern village of Talamone, north of Orbetello, Italy.

name: Temple to Nefertari at Abu Simbel
description: The smaller of the two temples at Abu Simbel, dedicated to the goddess Hathor and the deified queen Nefertari. The temple was relocated between 1964 and 1968 to prevent its submersion under Lake Nasser after the construction of the Aswan High Dam.

name: Camp celtique de la Bure
description: A Gallo-Roman settlement of a commercial nature located on the Meurthe river. Archaeological evidence for earthworks, as well as ritual activity, has been discovered. The Gallo-Roman ramparts, in a later phase, make use of marble sculpture as spolia.

name: Bard-i Nishande
description: Bard-i Nishande is the site of a ruined ancient landscape consisting of multiple complexes. According to K. Schippmann, these complexes include a ruler's residence or "castle", a terraced platform used for ritual activity, and a lower town.

props.ancientName: Bardneshāndeh, Bard Neshandeh, Bard-i Nishandi, Bard-è Néchandeh

name: Kai Ka'us
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 93 G3 Kai Ka'us

name: Khayrabad Bala
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 93 G3 Khayrabad Bala

name: Shami
description: Site in the Shami Valley (Khuzestan Province, Iran), a settlement or sanctuary of the Parthian period (ca. 2nd century BCE-2nd century CE). In 1936, a team led by Marc Aurel Stein uncovered a bronze sculpture (the "Shami statue") at the site.

name: Temple of "Artemis Epikrateia"
description: A classical/hellenistic temple located in the likely territory of Acarnanian Alyzeia. The temple site lies over 6km southeast from the ancient walled city (about a kilometer south of modern Paliovarka, Greece) at a site known in modern times as Drymonas Archontochoriou. It is perched on rising ground with a view westward to the channel and the island of Karnos (modern Kalamos). Inscriptions have been recovered from the site, and an unlooted portion of a votive deposit was scientifically excavated in the 1990s.

name: Ara della Regina
description: A fourth to third century B.C. sanctuary at Tarquinia.

name: Temple of Seti I
description: The Temple of Seti I at Abydos is also known as the "Great Temple of Abydos".

name: Temple of Ramesses II
description: The Temple of Ramesses II at Abydos in Egypt.

name: Aliphera sanctuary
description: Pausanias observed sanctuaries of both Asclepius and Athena at Aliphera, as well as an altar of Zeus Lecheates.

name: Deir el-Shelwit
description: A small temple dedicated to Isis on West bank of the Nile at Luxor. Its ancient name is unknown.

name: Sanctuary of Montélu
description: The Sanctuary of Montélu lies to the west of Chassenon. The building, only partially excavated, is of an octagonal plan and oriented to the cardinal points.

name: Bakcheion
description: A sanctuary to the west of the Acropolis of Athens, which was the clubhouse for a private religious association called the Iobakchoi, in the 2nd century AD.

name: Serapeum
description: The Serapeum in Ostia's Regio III was built between 123 and 126 CE thanks to private patronage.

name: Templum Veneris et Romae
description: A double temple sacred to Venus Felix and Roma Aeterna built by Hadrian on the slope of the Velian hill to the north of the Sacra Via beginning in 121 CE.

name: Temple of Hercules at Montorio al Vomano
description: A sanctuary of Hercules located in the territory of Teramo near the Vomano river.

name: Temple of Athena at Paestum
description: A Late Archaic Doric Temple of Athena (ca. 500 B.C.) at Paestum.

name: el-Garrusc
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 37 B1 el-Garrusc

name: Quarrush
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 37 B1 Quarrush

name: Bu Darag
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 Bu Darag

name: Gasr ar-Rakam
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Gasr ar-Rakam

name: Gasr Bu Zayd
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Gasr Bu Zayd

name: Gasr Zeitun
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Gasr Zeitun

name: Ruus el-Aslab
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Ruus el-Aslab

name: Serapeion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 B2 Serapeion

name: Wadi Senab
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 Wadi Senab

name: Temple at Asgafa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 no. 29 (Asgafa)

name: Minerva Cabardiacensis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 F4 Minerva Cabardiacensis

name: Monte S. Martino
description: The remains of a multi-period pre-Roman and Roman sanctuary.

name: Riva S. Vitale
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 D2 Riva S. Vitale

name: Temple of Hera, Olympia
description: The Temple of Hera at Olympia, also known as the Heraion, is a peripteral temple located in the northwest corner of the Altis precinct, dedicated to Hera. Built ca. 600-590 B.C., the Heraion is the oldest temple at Olympia and one of the earliest known examples of the Doric order in Greece.

name: Phanai
description: A large ancient sanctuary of Apollo, located on the southwest coast of the island of Chios.

name: Prepelaion?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E5 Prepelaion?

name: Skala Polykhnitou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Skala Polykhnitou

name: Smintheion
description: Homer mentions the temple of Apollo Smintheus as the shrine where Chryses was priest. The second-century B.C. remains of an Ionic sanctuary with an adjacent town are located in the southwestern Troad near modern Gülpinar.

name: Thea Larmene, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 H4 Thea Larmene, T.

name: Zeus Antigonios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 H3 Zeus Antigonios, T.

name: Apollo Korythos Sanctuary
description: A multi-phase sanctuary of Apollo that begins in the archaic period. Pausanias visited the sanctuary and observed two cult statues of the god. One of these, sacred to Apollo Corynthus, was of wood. The other, sacred to Apollo Argeotas, was of bronze.

name: Apollo Maleatas, T.
description: A cult of Apollo Maleatas has been identified at Profitis Ilias, Kosmas thanks to epigraphic evidence.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Limnatis on Mount Lapithos
description: This sanctuary, located near the modern village of Artemida in Elis (Greece), was excavated in the early 20th century.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Limnatis in the Taygetos
description: A sanctuary in the Taygetos mountains, attested in both literary and epigraphic texts, its precise location cannot yet be determined; however scattered and reused architectural components and inscriptions have been found in the area of the Chapel of Panagia Kapsocherovolousa, Volimos, northwest of modern Alagonia in the Peloponnese. Of uncertain foundation date, but confirmed by the Roman Senate under Tiberius as belonging to the Messenians (as part of the Ager Denthaliatis), rather than the Spartans.

name: Bassai
description: The Temple of Apollo Epikourios (often Latinized as Epicurius) at Bassae is an innovative mid-5th century B.C. Doric temple in Arcadia. The temple preserves the oldest Corinthian capital yet found. It was added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1986.

name: Didymoi
description: A place described by Pausanias who indicates that three sanctuaries are to be found here, namely those of Apollo, Poseidon, and Demeter.

name: Sanctuary of Eleusinian Demeter in Laconia
description: An extensive sanctuary mentioned by Pausanias and located at the modern village of Kalyvia Sochas by virtue of the discovery of a number of inscriptions on the site. Very little of the sanctuary is visible today, as the site was damaged by a number of floods and only ever partly excavated. Modern signage in the village marks the location of some finds.

name: Heraion
description: The Heraion of Perachora.

name: Heraion (Argolis)
description: The Argive Heraion was a major ancient sanctuary sacred to Hera located in the Argolid.

name: Hermai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 D3 Hermai

name: Hyperteleaton
description: Sanctuary of Apollo Hyperteleatas, partially excavated in 1885 and 1968. In ancient literature the sanctuary is only mentioned by Pausanias, in whose time it was called the Hyperteleaton and was dedicated to Asclepius. The site is located south of Phoiniki in the southeastern Peloponnese at a place known in the 19th century called Chasanaga.

name: Aphrodite sanctuary at Lokroi Epizephyrioi
description: The worship of Aphrodite is significant in the colonial city of Lokroi Epizephyrioi.

name: Temple E at Selinus
description: A fifth century B.C. Doric temple, sacred to perhaps Hera or Dionysus, located on the eastern hill at Selinus.

name: Temple of Demeter at the Sanctuary of Demeter Malophoros, Selinus
description: The Temple of Demeter was used for the worship of Demeter (Malophoros), goddess of fertility, grain, and agriculture. Built in the seventh century BC, the temple was used continuously until its abandonment ca. 250 BC

name: Ramesseum
description: The funerary temple of Ramesses II, built within the area known as the Ipet-isut, "The Most Selected of Places", along the Nile River.

name: Temple of Jupiter (Silifke)
description: A second-century CE temple sacred to Jupiter.

name: Santuari Sanniti del Monte Cila
description: The ruins of a sanctuary associated with the Samnites located on Monte Cila, where a megalithic fortification is also attested.

name: Sanctuary of Apollo Ismenios, Thebes
description: At Thebes, the foundations of a Doric temple dating to the Archaic and Classical periods were excavated by the American School of Classical Studies in 2015-6.

name: Dún Ailinne
description: An ancient ceremonial site located in County Kildare, Ireland. The circular enclosure once covered ca. 13 ha.

name: Asklepieion of Pheneos
description: The Asklepieion of Pheneos dates to the later second century BCE. It was identified via excavation in the late 1950s.

name: Temple of Ellesyia
description: A small rock-cut temple dedicated to Amun, Horus, and Satis dated to the 18th Dynasty. Originally located near Qasr Ibrim/Premis, the temple was relocated to the Museo Egizio in Turin, Italy, in the 1960s prior to the area's flooding beneath the surface of Lake Nasser.

name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: One of the four main Buddhist pilgrimage sites, identified as the place where Gautama Buddha attained Enlightenment. The first temple on the site was built by Emperor Asoka in the third century B.C., and the present temple dates from the fifth or sixth centuries. It is one of the earliest Buddhist temples built entirely in brick, still standing in India, from the late Gupta period. The site was largely abandoned from the twelfth-eighteenth centuries. It was named a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2002.

name: Frascole, S. Martino a Poggio
description: A stips votiva was discovered at the site of the Christian church of San Martino a Poggio - Frascole in 1887. The main ancient activity at the site likely took place during the middle of the third century B.C.

name: Temple of Roma and Augustus, Leptis Magna
description: The Temple of Roma and Augustus at Leptis Magna is a large temple (ca. 46 x 20 meters) situated on the so-called "Old Forum". It was initially erected between 8 BCE and 19 CE using limestone from Ras el-Hammam. The temple has a pseudo-peripteral temple and is oriented to the southeast. Reconstruction and restoration in the second century CE used marble instead of the original limestone.

props.ancientName: Tempel der Roma und des Augustus Leptis Magna

name: Capitolium of Terracina
description: A Tuscanic podium temple that is often referred to as being of the Capitolium type was built at Terracina in the middle of the first century BCE.

name: Tawern Roman temple complex
description: This reconstructed (in-situ) temple complex stands on a hill called Metzenberg, just to the southwest of modern Tawern in Germany. A partially excavated Gallo-Roman vicus lies at the foot of the hill, closer to town.

name: Re-Buried Hellenistic Temple and Workshops
description: Excavations on site of post office at 49 Ben Ami St. revealed Hellenistic remains, including a dedication to Zeus Soter on behalf of Antiochos VII by a courtier (SEG XIX 9094). This inscription was associated with Hellenistic architecture of a temple-like character. Nearby workshops also uncovered.

name: Beşdut
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 E3 Beşdut

name: Poseidon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 C2 Poseidon, T.

name: Sipahiler
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 C2 Sipahiler

name: Komosarue
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 M2 Komosarue

name: Satyrou Mnema
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 L2 Satyrou Mnema

name: Mut el-Kharab
description: The settlement of Mut el-Kharab, located in Egypt's Dakhleh Oasis. A temple to the god Seth was built at this location.

name: Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E3 Apollo, T.

name: Aksu - Zindan Mağarası Sanctuary
description: An ancient sanctuary complex, incorporating a cave known today as Zindan Mağarası, which overlooks a bend in the river Aksu (locally Zindan Deresi; a tributary of the Eurymedon) in Turkey's Isparta province. Archaeological and epigraphic evidence demonstrate human activity from the Hellenistic to the Seljuk period, with the dedication of a temple to the Vigenon Mother of the Gods during the reign of Marcus Aurelius. A Roman-era bridge and road section has also been identified as part of the complex, which was probably associated with the nearby city of Timbriada. The sanctuary also honored the god Eurymedon. A Christian monastery succeeded the sanctuary, spoliating its buildings for construction material.

name: Hephaistion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D5 Hephaistion

name: Letoon
description: Sanctuary of Leto and center of the Lycian federation.

name: Men Karou, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 A2 Men Karou, T.

name: Roma et Augustus, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D2 Roma et Augustus, T.

name: Phasiane Theos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 G2 Phasiane Theos

name: Hagar Qin Megalithic Temple Complex (Malta)
description: Ħaġar Qim is a megalithic temple complex (ca. 3600-3200 BC), included in the UNESCO World Heritage List.

name: Tas-Silġ
description: A hilltop on Malta that is the locus of multi-period settlement and religious activity from the Neolithic period to late antiquity.

name: Arx of Cosa
description: The citadel of the ancient city and location of its Capitolium.

name: Ešasurra
description: The temple of the god Išhara, Ešasurra (“House of the Womb”), was located in the Šuanna district (modern Ishin Aswad) of Babylon. According to the Babylonian topographical text Tintir = Babylon Tablet IV, this was one of two temples in that part of the city, which was located in the southern part of the eastern half of Babylon.

name: Temple of Vespasian
description: The "Temple of Vespasian" (VII, 9, 2), also referred to as Aedes Genii Augusti, is situated between the Temple of the Lares Publici and the Building of Eumachia on the eastern side of the forum of Pompeii. Its precise attribution is still a matter of debate.

name: Temple of Fortuna Augusta
description: A Corinthian order temple in Pompeii dedicated to Fortuna Redux and likely dating to the late first century BC (VII, 4, 1).

name: Area archeologica di Colle della Noce
description: The ancient temple in the sacred area of Colle della Noce dates to the fifth and fourth century BCE.

name: Sanctuary of Poseidon at Kalaureia, Poros
description: A sanctuary sacred to Poseidon on the island of Poros.

name: Chapel of Hakor
description: Chapel for the Sacred Barque of Amun-Ra in Karnak, established by the Egyptian pharaoh Hakor of the Twenty-ninth dynasty who reigned from 392/1 to 379/8 BCE.

name: Gallo-Roman fanum at Lapanouse-de-Cernon
description: The remains of a possible Gallo-Roman fanum were recovered, including an enclosure surrounding a rectangular structure along with terra sigillata from La Graufesenque.

name: Coventina's Well
description: A shrine to a Celtic water goddess situated near the Roman fort of Brocolitia at present-day Carrawburgh, along Hadrian's Wall.

name: Campochiaro
description: An Italic sanctuary sacred to Hercules located in località Civitella in the comune of Campochiaro. The site is to be connected with the Pentrian Samnites.

name: Temple of Maharraqa
description: A temple sacred to Isis and Osiris of Roman date, relocated before the completion of the Aswan dam in the 1960s.

name: Glava Panega
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B5 Glava Panega

name: Gura Dobrogei
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 F4 Gura Dobrogei

name: Kasnakovo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 C6 Kasnakovo

name: Demeter, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 F2 Demeter, T.

name: Gurzuf
description: According to Hind, a sanctuary dating ca. third century BC to second century AD was identified at the Gurzuf Pass. Silver and bronze statuettes of Graeco-Roman divinities were recovered.

name: Parthenion, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 G4 Parthenion, T.

name: Parthenion? Akron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 G4 Parthenion? Akron

name: New Year's Festival House at Assur
description: As part of his post-689-BC religious reforms, the Neo-Assyrian king Sennacherib (704–681 BC) had a new New Year’s festival house (akītu-house) constructed outside the western wall of Assur. This was an attempt to replicate the cultic topography of Babylon. The Sumerian ceremonial names of that holy building and its cella are Eabbaugga ("House Where Tiāmat Is Put To Death") and Edubdubabba ("House That Makes Tiāmat Tremble”) respectively.

name: Dilmun Temple at Saar
description: 20th century BCE Dilmun temple on the highest part of the ancient settlement mound at Saar in modern Bahrain.

name: Sri Ahobila Narasimha Swamy Temple
description: Main temple in Ahobilam dedicated to the man-lion form of the Hindu god Vishnu built or expanded in the 14th century, sacked by the Mughals in 1578, then restored and expanded at various times up to the present day.

name: Templum Sol
description: A sanctuary in the Campus Agrippae that was dedicated by Aurelian on December 25, 274, and funded by means of spoils taken from Palmyra.

name: Asklepieion and Lerna
description: A sanctuary complex on the northern margins of ancient Corinth near the theater.

props.ancientName: Temple of Asklepios and Lerna Spring

name: Apollo-Grannus-Tempel
description: A Roman temple in Faimingen near Lauingen in Bavaria.

name: Santuario rupestre di Silvano
description: The so-called rock-cut sanctuary of Silvanus at Terracina.

name: Asklepieion at Zea
description: A sanctuary of Asklepios above Zea harbour in Piraeus, founded in the late 5th century BC and active through the Imperial period. Sections of the sanctuary were explored archaeologically in the 1880s and 2000s.

name: Temple of Artemis Azzanathkona Phase 2
description: Temple at Dura-Europos dedicated to Artemis Azzanathkona, tutelary deity of the Euphrates island settlement Anath; building complex later used for Roman military record keeping in addition to worship.

name: Temple of Dionysos at Teos
description: A Temple of Dionysos in the Ionic order begun in the Hellenistic period.

name: Temple of Derr
description: A rock-cut temple (speos) located in Lower Nubia, the Temple of Derr was built by Pharaoh Ramesses II (19th Dynasty). The temple was relocated to its current position in 1964 along with the Temple of Amada.

name: Pachyammos sanctuary
description: The remains of a robbed-out Hellenistic temple complex were excavated at Pachyammos to the south of Paphos, Cyprus, between 2015 and 2019. The site included a peripteral temple in antis.

name: Qasr el-Aguz
description: Site in Luxor (Egypt) associated with a Ptolemaic temple dedicated to the god Thoth.

name: Eurmeiminanki
description: The ziggurat of the god the god Nabû at Borsippa. The Sumerian ceremonial name Eurmeiminanki means "House which Gathers the Seven (Me's) of Heaven and Underworld."

name: Elugalgalgasisa
description: The ziggurat of the god Sîn at Ur. The Sumerian ceremonial name Elugalgalgasisa means "House of the King who Lets Counsel Flourish". The platform temple was originally built during the Early Bronze Age and was then restored in the sixth century B.C. by King Nabonidus.

name: Ebabbar
description: The temple of the god Šamaš at Sippar. The Sumerian ceremonial name Ebabbar means "Shining House."

name: Temple B
description: A temple vowed by Q. Lutatius Catulus at the battle of Vercellae (30 June, 101 B.C.). The sanctuary is a tholos-type temple and was sometimes referred to as 'aedes Catuli'.

name: Sanctuary of Kodros, Neleus and Basile at Athens
description: A sanctuary located between the Phaleron gate and the Itonian gate of Athens.

props.ancientName: Sanctuary of Kodros and Neleus and Basile

name: Bouri cult site (Megara)
description: An Archaic coastal sanctuary located at Alepochori, Megara, Greece.

name: Giza
description: The site of Giza encompasses the Giza Necropolis, a large archaeological site famous for its pyramid complexes and monumental sculpture.

name: Temple of Nabu at Palmyra
description: A temple dedicated to the Babylonian god of wisdom.

name: Temple of Thelsae
description: A well-preserved Roman period temple located in modern Dmeir, Syria. The sanctuary was later converted into a fortification.

name: Sanctuary of the People and Graces
description: An open air sanctuary in Athens, dedicated to the People (Demos) of Athens and the Graces (Charites), located between the Dipylon Gate and the Agora. Archaeological remains were partially destroyed during construction of the Athens-Piraeus railway

props.ancientName: Sanctuary of the People and Graces

name: Sanctuary of Bendis
description: Sanctuary of Bendis located on the Acropolis of Salamis

name: Temple of Jupiter Anxur
description: A late Republican sanctuary of the city of Terracina that is typically dated ca. 100-70 B.C.

name: Spathari (temple)
description: A temple in Acarnania, located between Stratos (modern Sourovigli) and modern Lepenou. The temple seems to have been constructed in the Greek Archaic period on the site of an earlier Helladic settlement. The temple continued in operation into the Roman period.

name: Sanctuary of Aphrodite/Isis
description: A sanctuary on the south slope of the Acropolis of Athens, dedicated to Aphrodite in the Classical period and associated with Isis from the late first century BC. A temple was built in the Hadrianic period.

name: Triangular Shrine
description: A triangular shrine located at a crossroads to the southwest of the Athenian Agora, probably dedicated to a hero, built in the late fifth century BC with evidence for earlier religious activity in the seventh century BC.

name: Temple of Apollo at Corinth
description: The second temple of Apollo at Corinth whose reconstructed remains are visible on the site today. The structure dates ca. 540 B.C.

name: Mithraeum at Vulci
description: A mithraeum was discovered at Vulci in 1975 during excavations of a Roman aristocratic house. The shrine was well preserved with two tauroctony scenes dating to the third century. The site was destroyed in the fourth century A.D.

name: So-called Temple of the Dioscuri
description: A temple on the acropolis of Cyrene, probably built around the seventh century BCE.

name: So-called Temple of Cybele
description: A recently discovered temple on the acropolis of Cyrene.

name: So-called Temple of Aphrodite
description: A temple located on the acropolis of Cyrene.

name: Temple East of Lavinium
description: From the 5th century BC.

name: Unlabeled feature captured
description: Unlabeled feature captured from the Barrington Atlas

name: Sanctuary of Pan at Paneas
description: The Paneion at Caesarea Philippi, a sanctuary site dedicated to Pan centered around a natural cave and spring site on the southwestern slopess of Mount Hermon. The sanctuary was first built in the Hellenistic period (before 200 BCE). A temple to Augustus was constructed in front of the cave during the reign of Herod the Great in 19 BCE, and the site was further expanded through 220 CE.

name: Cerreto Sannita
description: A Samnite temple dedicated to Flora.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Munychia (Piraeus)
description: A now mostly destroyed ancient temple dedicated to Artemis Munychia on the promontory above Mikrolimani. A Yacht Club in the 1930s was built on top of the promontory.

name: Acropolis of Cumae
description: The Acropolis of Cumae was used across multiple periods, beginning with the foundation of the Greek colony in the eighth century B.C.

name: Macchia delle Valli, Vetralla
description: A rock-cut Etrusco-Roman sanctuary sacred to Demeter that was discovered during rescue excavations in 2006 following looting activity. The sanctuary, set in a grove, remained in use until the second century A.D.

name: Vigna Parrocchiale archaeological area
description: A complex of civic and sacred buildings in the urban area of ancient Caere, including the so-called "elliptical building".

name: Filitosa
description: Filitosa is a megalithic site in southern Corsica that was in use from the Neolithic period until Roman times. A number of menhirs have been discovered at the site.

name: Temple of Aphrodite (Thessalonica)
description: The remains of a marble temple executed in the Ionic order.

name: Temple of Apollo at Thermos
description: A temple to the Apollo was built in the late seventh century B.C. atop the ruins of the previous megaron in Thermos.

name: Kiosk of Qertassi
description: The so-called Kiosk of Qertassi is an unfinished Roman kiosk that may date to the reign of Trajan. Originally located at Qertassi, the remains of the temple were relocated to New Kalabsha in Southern Egypt as part of the International Campaign to Save the Monuments of Nubia in the twentieth century.

name: Yeha Temple
description: The Yeha Temple is alleged to be among the oldest known extant structures in Ethiopia. It is executed in the Sabaean style and may date to the seventh century BCE.

name: Sanctuary at Leben
description: Leben is known for a sanctuary of Asklepios dating to the first and second centuries CE.

name: Temple of Iuno Moneta at Signia
description: The remains of the Temple of Iuno Moneta on the acropolis of Segni support the later (tenth century) church of St. Peter.

props.ancientName: Tempel der Juno Moneta, Juno Moneta (Tempel der)

name: el Hofra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F4 el Hofra

name: es-Soumaa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F4 es-Soumaa

name: Pagus Assalitanus
description: Pagus Assalitanus, located in Africa Proconsularis, lies some 6 km southeast of Thubursicu Bure.

name: Saturnus Balcaranensis, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 F3 Saturnus Balcaranensis, T.

name: Sidi-Mechrig
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D2 Sidi-Mechrig

name: Ain Dara temple
description: The Ain Dara temple, an archaeological site located near the village of Ain Dara, in Afrin, Syria, was the location of an Iron Age Syro-Hittite temple. The structure was noted for the similarities it bore to Solomon's Temple in Jerusalem. The site was in use from ca. 1300 until 740 BCE. It finds comparison with the temples at Emar, Mumbaqa, and Ebla's Temple D. In January 2018 the ruins of the temple were substantially damaged by air strikes carried out by the Turkish Air Force during the Afrin offensive. The site also subsequently suffered further damage, wanton destruction, and looting.

name: Kordin Temples
description: A group of megalithic temples on Malta constructed during the Neolithic period.

name: Cancho Roano
description: A pre-Roman sanctuary of the Iberian peninsula.

name: Sanctuary of Sol Indiges
description: The remains of the Sanctuary of Sol Indiges are located near Pomezia, Italy. The original phase of the sanctuary dates to the last quarter of the sixth century BCE, with subsequent rebuilding in the late fourth century BCE.

name: Sarapieion C at Delos
description: One of three shrines sacred to Serapis identified by archaeologists working at Delos in the nineteenth century.

name: Sarapieion A
description: The earliest of the three shrines sacred to Serapis identified by archaeologists working at Delos in the nineteenth century.

name: Temple of Fortuna (Jublains)
description: Centerpoint of sanctuary at modern Jublains

name: Temple of Thutmose III
description: The Temple of Thutmose III at Deir el-Bahari was built ca. 1435-1425 BCE.

name: Bona Dea Subsaxana, Aedes
description: An ancient sanctuary of the Aventine Hill in Rome.

name: Temple of Baalshamin
description: Temple in Palmyra dedicated to the Canaanite god Baalshamin.

name: Tempio di Grasceta dei Cavallari
description: The temple of Grasceta dei Cavallari is an Etrusco-Roman sanctuary located in the municipality of Tolfa. This sanctuary sits at the foot of Mount Mazza, above a pass near Tarquinia and Caere's ancient borders. The temple was small, with a rectangular platform and an octastyle arrangement.

name: Saint-Sernin-sur-Rance
description: Remains of a rustic temple and a menhir were discovered in the early 1960s near Boutaran.

name: Bayon at Angkor Thom
description: A temple in the Khmer capital of Angkor Thom, constructed in the late 12th or early 13th century CE.

name: Temple of Apollo (Side)
description: A Roman temple at Side dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius.

name: Zar Trypa Cave Sanctuary
description: A cave sanctuary, dedicated to the Nymphs, located on the east side of Mount Ossa's western "Psila Dendra" ridge north of the modern village of Spilia. Epigraphic and other evidence indicates the sanctuary was active from the Classical through Roman periods.

name: Pigades
description: A Late Bronze Age settlement and sanctuary.

name: Doric temple
description: A temple of the Doric order (VIII,7,30-34) located in the so-called Triangular Forum of Pompeii.

name: Tabard Square Temple Complex
description: A Romano-Celtic temple complex located near the junction of Stane Street and Watling Street in modern Southwark.

name: Marasà Sud
description: An ancient sanctuary dedicated to Aphrodite located at Locri Epizephyrii.

name: Merjiyat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B2 Merjiyat

name: Nebi Safa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Nebi Safa

name: Qafr Zebad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Qafr Zebad

name: Qasr Neba
description: Qasr Neba is the site of a Roman temple in the Bekaa valley of Lebanon.

name: Qasr Nemrud
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 D2 Qasr Nemrud

name: Zekweh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Zekweh

name: Marisa
description: A Judean, Edomite, and later Hellenistic settlement. Destroyed ca. 40 BCE by the Parthians.

name: Baron-sur-Odon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 F2 Baron-sur-Odon

name: Oisseau-Le-Petit
description: Oisseau-Le-Petit is the site of a Gallo-Roman fanum.

name: St-Aubin-sur-Mer
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 F2 St-Aubin-sur-Mer

name: Khirbet et-Tannur
description: Located some 70 km from Petra, Khirbet et-Tannur is the site of a well-preserved, open-air Nabataean sanctuary.

name: el-Qasr
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 71 B3 el-Qasr

name: Ag. Eirini
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B2 Ag. Eirini

name: Ag. Tykhonas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Ag. Tykhonas

name: Aphrodite Akraia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 F1 Aphrodite Akraia, T.

name: Aphrodite Golgia
description: An important sanctuary of Aphrodite in the ancient city of Golgoi on the island of Cyprus.

name: Aphrodite Paphia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Aphrodite Paphia, T.

name: Aphrodite, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Aphrodite, T.

name: Aphrodites Lophos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E3 Aphrodites Lophos

name: Apollo Agyates, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Apollo Agyates, T.

name: Apollo Alasiotas, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Apollo Alasiotas, T.

name: Apollo Hylates, T.
description: The temple of Apollo Hylates, located northwest of Kourion, was one of the most important cult centers of ancient Cyprus. The site was active from the eighth century BCE and until the fourth century CE.

name: Apollo Mageirios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Apollo Mageirios, T.

name: Apollo Melanthios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Apollo Melanthios, T.

name: Ayios Photios
description: Caches of sculpture discovered in the 19th century by Luigi di Cesnola provide evidence for a cult of a male deity in the area of the modern Ayios Photios church near Athienou on Cyprus.

name: Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Apollo, T.

name: Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D3 Apollo, T.

name: Achna
description: Achna is a modern village on Cyprus where evidence exists for an ancient sanctuary dedicated to a goddess perhaps associated with Artemis.

name: Athena, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Athena, T.

name: Baal-Hamman, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Baal-Hamman, T.

name: Geronisos
description: A small island lying off the west coast of Cyprus where a sanctuary has been identified.

name: Hera, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Hera, T.

name: Kapsalaes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Kapsalaes

name: Komissariato
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Komissariato

name: Laneia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Laneia

name: Laxia tis Skhistis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B2 Laxia tis Skhistis

name: Mavrovounoi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Mavrovounoi

name: Myrtou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Myrtou

name: Nymphaion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Nymphaion

name: Pergamos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Pergamos

name: Rantidi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Rantidi

name: Toumpa
description: Iron Age sanctuary site located approximately 4.5 km south of the city of Salamis.

name: Trakhonas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Trakhonas

name: Vounari
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Vounari

name: Zeus Labranios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Zeus Labranios, T.

name: Leuke Akte
description: Leuke Akte, the ‘White cape’ located on the Libyan coast of Egypt, was the site of an Isis cult wherein the Egyptian goddess was worshipped as Aphrodite, Muchis, and Eseremphis. An oracular shrine of Apollo was also found there.

name: el-Muftella
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: el Qassabat el-Gharbiya
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: Umm Ubaydah
description: An ancient place, cited: None

name: Deir Abou Menas
description: An ancient Christian pilgrimage site that grew into an active town centered around the monastery complex devoted to Saint Menas who is said to have been buried there. The town reached its peak as the most visited pilgrimage site in Egypt during the sixth century AD.

props.ancientName: Deir Abou Menas, Deir Abu Mena, Deir Abu Menas, Abu Mina

name: Caesareum of Palmyra
description: The complex, identified as the Caesareum of Palmyra, was converted into a mosque during the Umayyad period.

name: Sanctuary of Poseidon (Onchestos)
description: Pausanias describes a Sanctuary of Poseidon located at Onchestos that preserved a grove sacred to the god and that Homer had once praised.

props.ancientName: naos kai agalma Poseidonos Onchestiouναός καὶ ἄγαλμα Ποσειδῶνος Ὀγχηστίου

name: Sanctuary of Aphrodite Ourania
description: A sanctuary of Aphrodite Ourania ("Heavenly") in Athens, on the Panathenaic Way as it entered the Athenian Agora. It consisted of an altar built around 500 BC and a temple built in the early first century AD. The Late Roman Stoa was built over the top of it in the fifth century AD.

props.ancientName: hieròn Aphrodítēs Ouranías, hieron Aphrodites Ouranias

name: Temple of Kronos and Rhea
description: A sanctuary dedicated to Kronos and Rhea in the area of the Olympieion in Athens, attested from Classical times into the Roman Imperial period. A 2nd-century AD Doric temple is probably associated with it.

props.ancientName: naòs Krónou kaì Réas, naos Kronou kai Rheas

name: Grotto of the Sibyl
description: A cave discovered by Amedeo Maiuri in 1932 and connected in the historical tradition with the Cumaean Sibyl. The site features in the sixth book of Virgil's Aeneid.

name: Temple of Augustus
description: The Temple of Augustus in Pula is a Roman temple that dates to the time of Augustus. The tetrastyle Corinthian temple sites a high podium.

name: Sanctuary of Erynike Aphrodite
description: A sanctuary of Aphrodite Erykine near Psophis in Achaia that is mentioned by Pausanias.

name: Asklepeion of Ancient Trichoneion
description: An excavated sanctuary sacred to Asklepios located at Gavalou in Aitoloakarnania West Greece.

name: Velletri-Stimmate
description: At Velletri the remains of a Late Archaic sanctuary (ca. 530 BCE) were discovered beneath the church of the SS. Stimmate di S. Francesco. The site is often referred to as "Tempio delle Stimmate"; Cardinal Stefano Borgia discovered it in 1784. The architectural terracottas from the sanctuary are part of the so-called "Veio-Roma-Velletri" decorative system.

name: Funerary temple of Ay and Horemheb
description: Temple dedicated to the cult of Kings Ay and Horemheb located on the west bank of Thebes.

props.ancientName: Funerary temple of Ay and Horemheb

name: Ponte delle Monache sanctuary
description: A Campanian sanctuary located in the territory of Cales in Campania.

name: Karnasion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 B3 Karnasion

name: Capitolium of Cosa
description: The so-called Capitolium temple of the Latin colony of Cosa.

name: Khorsabad ziggurat
description: The eighth-century-BC Assyrian king Sargon II (r. 721–705 BC) had a temple-tower built on the palace terrace of his new capital, Dūr-Šarrukīn (modern Khorsabad). The ziggurat was located at the northern end of the temple sector, which was accessible from Sargon’s palace, through the Courtyard XV.

name: Housesteads Mithraeum
description: Temple of Mithras at Roman fort of Housesteads (Vercovicium)

name: Santuario di Fondo Patturelli
description: A Samnite sanctuary of ancient Capua that was initially discovered in 1845. Active from the sixth century B.C. until the first century B.C., the Santuario di Fondo Patturelli is noted for its architectural terracotta as well as for the presence of a sacred maternal cult.

name: Lucus Furrinae
description: The Lucus Furrinae or Syrian Sanctuary is a sanctuary site on the Janiculum Hill in Rome. The first known construction at the site took place during the late first century CE, and it remained in use until the mid-fourth century CE.

name: Temple of Artemis at Ephesus
description: The Temple of Artemis, the central element of the extramural sanctuary of Artemis at Ephesus, was considered one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

name: Temple district at Hattusa
description: An area of the upper city of Hattusha with the remains of up to 30 temples.

name: Temple of Augustus, Barcelona
description: The Temple of Augustus dates to the second century A.D.

name: The Temple of Athena Polias at Priene
description: The temple of Athena at Priene, designed by the architect Pytheos, is an especially good example of the Late Classical form of the Ionic order.

name: Temple of Iuno Sospita, Lanuvium
description: A celebrated cult-place sacred to Iuno Sospita that retained its sacred significance even after a cult to the same divinity was dedicated in the Forum Holitorium in Rome in 194 BCE.

name: Temple G at Selinus
description: Remains of an octastyle Doric temple begun in the sixth century B.C. but left unfinished.

name: Andrianton
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D2 Andrianton

name: Labyrinthos
description: Labyrinthos is a name applied by Greek authors, notably Herodotus and Strabo, to describe a monumental funerary temple at Hawara.

name: Jebel Moneijah
description: Jebel Moneijah was the site of a Nabatean sanctuary in the Southern Sinai.

name: Risqah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 G3 Risqah

name: Abydos/Ebot
description: An ancient Egyptian city and the burial place of its first kings. Primary cult place of the god Osiris.

name: Temple of Amun at Jebel Barkal
description: Large temple dedicated to the Amun located at the base of Jebel Barkal. Designated temple B500, the structure was likely constructed by the Napatan king Piankhi in the 8th century BCE.

name: Aigaion Antron
description: A cave on Crete sacred to the Minoans that may be the Diktaean cave, the putative site of Zeus' birth in Greek mythology.

name: Apollo Erethimios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 G3 Apollo Erethimios, T.

name: Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 A1 Apollo, T.

name: Ares, Aphrodite, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 E2 Ares, Aphrodite, T.

name: Athena, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 F2 Athena, T.

name: Diktynnaion
description: A sanctuary located at Menies bay in Chania, Crete, with remains ranging in date from Hellenistic period to Late Antiquity.

name: Eileithyias Antron
description: The Cave of Eileithyia outside Heraklion on Crete is an ancient sanctuary sacred to the goddess of childbirth. The shrine was active during Neolithic, Minoan, and Mycenaean phases.

name: Idaion Antron
description: A sacred cave on Crete where it was believed Rhea hid Zeus to protect him from Cronus. Archaeological evidence indicates use as a shelter or dwelling as early as the Late Neolithic period, with religious activities taking over in the Bronze Age and continuing through the Roman period.

name: Kranaion Antron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 C2 Kranaion Antron

name: Lera
description: An ancient cave sanctuary on the Akrotiri peninsula of Crete.

name: Panagia Arkoudiotissa
description: The Arkoudiotissa Cave on Crete has been the site of ritual activity since antiquity. The cave's cultic association stems from a rock formation that looks like a bear (Arkoudiotissa ("she-bear")).

name: Pigi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 D2 Pigi

name: Temple of Poseidon and Amphitrite on Tenos
description: A peripteral temple.

name: Tallaion Antron
description: The Melidoni Cave - Gerontospilios.

name: Theai kai Nymphai (temple)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 D5 Theai kai Nymphai, T.

name: Zeus Atabyrios (temple)
description: A sanctuary dedicated to Zeus Atabyrios located on the summit of Mt. Atavyros in Rhodes.

name: Zeus Thenatas, T.
description: Zeus Thenatas, T. is an archaic Greek sanctuary built atop earlier Minoan cult remains. The open-air temple remained in use until the second century AD.

name: Sanctuary of Apollo on Delos
description: The famous sanctuary of Apollo on the Cycladic island of Delos.

name: Archilocheion
description: A sanctuary dedicated to the Greek lyric poet Archilochus, known as the Archilocheion, was established on Paros during the third century BCE. It is attested textually in both literature and inscriptions; however, the precise location of the sanctuary has not been identified. During the 19th and 20th centuries, some scholars argued for it to be placed at the site of the “Three Churches” (Tris Ekklisies) because inscriptions mentioning Archilochos were found reused in remains of the 6th century Christian basilica over which the later churches had been built. Other scholars (e.g., Kontoleon in PECS) have vigorously rejected this view. Foss (BAtlDir) cites Berranger in tentatively placing the Archilocheion further east at the modern village of Elitas.

name: Artemis Parthenos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D3 Artemis Parthenos, T.

name: Artemis Tauropolos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 C2 Artemis Tauropolos, T.

name: Artemision
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Artemision

name: Asklepieion (Kos)
description: A major, ancient sanctuary of Asklepios located on the Aegean island of Kos.

name: Asklepieion, Thesmophorion?, Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Asklepieion, Thesmophorion?, Apollo, T.

name: Aspripetra
description: A cavernous grotto on the island of Kos that seems to have served as a place of worship in ancient times. Pan and the Nymphs were venerated there in Archaic Greek times. The grotto's ancient name is unknown.

name: Athena Poliouchos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Athena Poliouchos, T.

name: Athena, Zeus?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A5 Athena, Zeus?, T.

name: Delion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Delion

name: Didyma/Branchidai/Ioustinianopolis
description: The ancient sanctuary of Apollo near Miletos (modern Didim, Turkey).

name: Eleusinioi Theoi, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 Eleusinioi Theoi, T.

name: Gyroulas
description: A Late Archaic rural sanctuary of Demeter and Apollo at Gyroulas on Naxos.

name: Heraion
description: The sanctuary of Hera on Samos.

name: Hyria (Naxos)
description: A sixth century B.C. sanctuary located on the island of Naxos.

name: Khrysomilia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 C2 Khrysomilia

name: Klaros
description: The site of a temple of Apollo administered by Colophonians and active from the seventh century B.C. to the fourth century A.D.

name: Meter Theon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A5 Meter Theon, T.

name: Panionion
description: Located near Mt. Mycale, the Panionion was an Ionian sanctuary dedicated to Poseidon Helikonios that served as the meeting place of the Ionian League.

name: Poseidon Phytalmios?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A4 Poseidon Phytalmios?, T.

name: Poseidon?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Poseidon?, T.

name: Sanctuary of Poseidon Hippios (Mantineia)
description: Sanctuary to Poseidon at Mantineia, near the village of Milia. According to Pausanias, it was located near the Arnis spring.

name: Sanctuary of the Dioscouri at Athens
description: Also known as the Anakeion, located likely East of the Acropolis.

name: Temple of Hera I (the "Basilica")
description: An Archaic Doric temple probably dedicated to Hera in the central urban sanctuary of Paestum/Poseidonia.

props.ancientName: Tempio di Hera detto la Basilica

name: Terrace and altar of M. Nonius Balbus
description: Marcus Nonius Balbus of Nuceria was an important civic patron of Herculaneum. Balbus was a praetor and proconsul of Crete and Cyrene and served as tribune of the plebs in 32 B.C. This architectural complex near the Suburban Baths included the terrace as well as an honorific statue and altar.

name: Amphitheater of the Three Gauls
description: The Sanctuary of the Three Gauls (Tres Galliae) was a late first century B.C. sanctuary in Lugdunum; the amphitheater dates to 19 B.C.

props.ancientName: Amphithéâtre des Trois Gaules, Amphitheatre des Trois Gaules

name: Temple of the Severan family at Cuicul
description: A temple dedicated to the genius of the Severan family.

name: Šarrat-niphi temple (Kalhu)
description: When Kalhu (biblical Calah, modern Nimrud) became the principal administrative center of the Assyrian Empire in the ninth century BC, king Ashurnasirpal II (r. 883–859 BC) built (or rebuilt) numerous temples. One of those was dedicated to the goddess Šarrat-niphi (an aspect of Ištar).

name: Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III
description: Temple constructed in the Theban Necropolis during the 18th Dynasty.

name: Temple of Dakka
description: An ancient temple relocated in the 1960s before the completion of the Aswan dam.

name: Anaktoron at Pantalika
description: The Anaktoron is an enigmatic megalithic building associated with the prehistoric Sicilian settlement of Pantalica; its polygonal blocks may date to the 12-11th c. B.C., but the current form of the building seems to be the result of Byzantine reconstruction.

name: Aphrodision
description: Located outside the city walls of Pergamon, this temple, according to an inscription (OGIS 273-9), was supposedly the site of a victory of Attalos I over the Tolistogi, Galatians, and Antiochus Hierax. It was likely later destroyed by Philip V.

name: Göbekli Tepe
description: The site of Göbekli Tepe, located in the Germuş mountains of south-eastern Anatolia, was the location of a Neolithic hilltop sanctuary. This sanctuary, thought to be a series of stone enclosures with T-shaped pillars carrying carving of wild animals, was built in the Pre-Pottery Neolithic and ranges in date from 9,600 to 8,200 B.C.

name: Temple of Castor and Pollux (Rome)
description: Located at the southeast corner of the Forum Romanum, the aedes Castoris is a temple of ancient Rome vowed by the dictator Postumius in 499 BCE.

name: Asklepieion at Eleusis
description: A sanctuary of Asklepios at Eleusis, established in the late 5th century BC and active through the Imperial era. It is known from finds of inscriptions and sculpture to have been located somewhere north of the sanctuary of Demeter and Kore, but the exact site is lost.

name: 'Ein Gedi
description: A Ghassulian public building dating ca. 3500 B.C. near the oasis of Ein Gedi on the western shore of the Dead Sea.

name: Gallo-Roman fanum at Recoules-Prévinquières
description: The fanum of a Gallo-Roman cult that was in use from the first century B.C. to the fourth century A.D.

name: Trophonius oracle (Boeotia)
description: An oracular cave sanctuary at Lebadeia (modern Greece). Pausanias reports seeing a cult image here that was made by Praxiteles.

name: Temple of Herakles, Cora
description: The Temple of Herakles at Cora was built during the 80s B.C. The temple is tetrastyle in its plan and built following the Etrusco-Italic temple tradition.

name: Ningal temple (Khorsabad)
description: As part of the palatial complex of Dūr-Šarrukīn (modern Khorsabad), the eighth-century-BC Assyrian king Sargon II (r. 721–705 BC) constructed six temples, including one to the goddess Ningal.

name: Adad temple (Khorsabad)
description: As part of the palatial complex of Dūr-Šarrukīn (modern Khorsabad), the eighth-century-BC Assyrian king Sargon II (r. 721–705 BC) constructed six temples, including one to the god Adad.

name: Šamaš temple (Khorsabad)
description: As part of the palatial complex of Dūr-Šarrukīn (modern Khorsabad), the eighth-century-BC Assyrian king Sargon II (r. 721–705 BC) constructed six temples, including one to the god Šamaš.

name: Group of monuments at Mahabalipuram (Q3522747)
description: This group of sanctuaries was carved out of rock along the Coromandel coast in the seventh and eighth centuries. The Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: Banteay Srei
description: Buddhist temple at Angkor, located approximately 20 km northeast of the main temple complex. Dedicated to Shiva, the temple was constructed during the 10th century CE during the reigns of Rajendravarman II and Jayavarman V.

name: Monte Papalucio
description: The sanctuary of Monte Papalucio was an important Messapian sanctuary. It is located on the mountain of the same name in the territory of Oria in the province of Brindisi. The deities Demeter and Persephone were venerated in this cave sanctuary.

name: Hephaistia
description: An ancient settlement on the northern coast of the Greek island of Lemnos.

name: Herakles, T.
description: A sanctuary of Herakles located on the island of Lemnos, active from the Archaic period through to the time of the Roman empire.

name: Kabeireion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 A2 Kabeireion

name: Khontro Bigli
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Khontro Bigli

name: Meter Theon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 Meter Theon, T.

name: Metokhi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C5 Metokhi

name: Monasteraki
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Monasteraki

name: Nagos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C4 Nagos

name: Sanctuary of Iuno Gabina
description: The Sanctuary of Iuno Gabina at Gabii.

name: Unnamed temple (Tre Fontane)
description: M. Cristofani describes terracotta antefixes discovered at Tre Fontane, in the general location of the abbey of the same name. These antefixes form part of a set of evidence relevant to a discussion of the functioning of regional coroplastic workshops in central Italy in the late archaic period. They are dated from the later sixth to the early fifth centuries BCE.

name: Unnamed temple near Solforata
description: An ancient temple located near Solforata, between Lavinium and the Alban Lake in modern Italy.

name: Temple near Buon Riposo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C3 no. 52 (temple near Ardea)

name: Temple at Algidus M.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D3 no. 56 (temple at Algidus M.)

name: Sanctuary near Villamagna
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 E3 no. 61 (cult place near Trerus fl.)

name: Unnamed temple
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 E2 no. 62 (temple NW Anagnia)

name: Angitiae Lucus
description: Angitiae Lucus refers to the grove of Angitia, a divine figure of the Marsi tribe.

name: Apollo et Nymphae Nitrodes (temple)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E4 Apollo et Nymphae Nitrodes, T.

name: Atessa/Porcari Sanctuary
description: A small Italic sanctuary, possibly of Vejovis, in a modern area known then as Passo Porcari (between Monte Marcone and Saletti) near Atessa in Italy's Chieti province. It was excavated in the 1970s and is memorialized by the modern street name "Via Veiove". Rescue excavations of purportedly medieval finds were carried out in the immediate vicinity between 2017 and 2020 ahead of a road modification.

name: Cocullo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E1 Cocullo

name: Colle Sparanise
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G2 Colle Sparanise

name: Diana Tifatina (temple)
description: The Fanum Dianae Tifatinae located on the slopes of Mt. Tifata in Campania.

name: ‘Herculis Rani’?
description: A large fanum sacred to ‘Herculis Rani’? at Campochiaro, the name of which is then preserved in the station mentioned on the Peutinger Table. Scholars like Adriano La Regina support linking this fanum to a place mentioned by Livy, although see the discussion by Oakley.

name: Lucus Feroniae
description: A sanctuary of Feronia located at a spring on the via Appia near Tarracina.

name: Madonnna del Canneto
description: The sanctuary site of Madonnna del Canneto is located at Settefrati within the confines of the Abruzzo national park in Italy. It has a long history as a sacred site, beginning with sacred activities dedicated to Mefitis in the later first millennium BCE.

name: Minerva temple at Punta della Campanella
description: An ancient temple of Minerva, located on the ancient cape that also was known by the goddess' name.

name: Monte del Fico
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E2 Monte del Fico

name: Panetelle
description: A sanctuary with votive deposits dating to the Hellenistic period at Panetelle di Mondragone.

name: Samnite Sanctuary at Pietrabbondante
description: An ancient sanctuary on the slope of Monte Saraceno in the modern Italian comune of Pietrabbondante.

name: Pyrgi
description: Pyrgi is an ancient Etruscan port city on the Tyrrhenian coast of Italy. A Roman colony was established at the site by 191 B.C. In 1964, the bilingual 'Pyrgi Plaques' were discovered during the course of excavations.

name: Roccaspromonte
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G2 Roccaspromonte

name: S. Giovanni in Galdo
description: Site of a small Samnitic sanctuary atop Colle Rimontato (709 m. a.s.l.)

name: Scanno
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E2 Scanno

name: Schiavi
description: Twin Italic temples located atop the Colle della Torre.

name: Sirenae, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Sirenae, T.

name: Tarracina(e)/Anxur
description: A strategic Volscian city that had come under Roman control by 509 B.C.; a Roman citizen colony was established there in 329 B.C. The site is particularly notable for the remains of the terraced sanctuary of Iuppiter Anxur.

name: Terravecchia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G3 Terravecchia

name: The Sanctuary of Vastogirardi
description: A sanctuary of the Pentrian Samnites on the flanks of Monte Capraro, north of the modern Italian village of Vastogirardi.

name: Albanella
description: A rural sanctuary in the territory of Poseidonia (Paestum) has been excavated at S. Nicola di Albanella.

name: Capaccio
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B4 Capaccio

name: Capodifiume
description: Remains of a fourth-century B.C. sanctuary situated at a spring known as Caput aquae, from which the modern toponym is most likely derived.

name: Casalbore
description: At modern Casalbore, Italy, archaeological evidence includes a significant temple structure and associated material from the Samnite and Roman periods.

name: Drion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C1 Drion

name: Grotta Trinità
description: The Grotta Trinità is a cave sanctuary located in the comune of Ruffano, province of Lecce, in southern Italy. The cave was in use from the Neolithic period onwards. In the Iron Age, terracotta vessels with incised graffiti date activity to the sixth century BCE and subsequent.

name: Temple of Hera at Foce del Sele
description: A sanctuary at the mouth of the Sele river in Italy with an octastyle temple dedicated to the Argive Hera.

name: Hera temple
description: The so-called 'Tavole Palatine' is the site of the ruins of a sixth century B.C. Doric temple sacred to Hera and associated with the city of Metapontum.

name: Lincietti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B1 Lincietti

name: Mefitis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B3 Mefitis

name: Oscata di Sopra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B2 Oscata di Sopra

name: Piano dei Tivoli
description: Archaeological excavations conducted at the locality known as Piano dei Tivoli revealed, by means of numerous fragments of architectural terracottas, a sanctuary of the Hellenistic period. There is also evidence of settlement during the fifth and fourth centuries BCE.

name: Pizzica
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E4 Pizzica

name: Porto Cesareo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 G4 Porto Cesareo

name: Roccadaspide
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B4 Roccadaspide

name: Rossano
description: Rossano di Vaglio, a fourth century BC site dedicated to Mefitis, was a Lucanian federal sanctuary.

name: Ruoti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Ruoti

name: Mortuary Temple of Senwosret III
description: Mortuary temple constructed for Senwosret III, a pharaoh of the 12th Dynasty in Egypt.

name: Antikythera Apollo Sanctuary
description: The probable sanctuary of Apollo Aegileos on the modern Aegean island of Antikythera is attested by the presence of a squared marble platform and various artifacts, including sculptural remains.

name: Temple of Juno (Hera Lacinia)
description: A temple of the Doric order that was built at Akragas ca. 450 BCE.

name: Sanctuary of Fortuna Primigenia
description: The Sanctuary of Fortuna Primigenia at Praeneste is a terraced sanctuary of monumental proportions. The phase visible today dates to the late second century B.C.

name: Second Temple, Jerusalem
description: The so-called "Second Temple" in Jerusalem stood on the northern edge of the Dome of the Rock and was destroyed in A.D. 70 during the Roman siege.

name: Temple of Apollo Pythios
description: The Hellenistic Temple of Apollo Pythios on the Acropolis of the city of Rhodes.

name: Temple of Zeus at Olympia
description: The Temple of Zeus at Olympia was built in the second quarter of the fifth century B.C. Libon of Elis served as architect.

name: Roman Temple of Évora
description: The Roman Temple of Évora, also referred to as the Temple of Diana, is a Roman temple of the first century A.D.

name: Shrine of Venus Cloacina
description: The Sacrum Cloacina was a small shrine dedicated to Venus Cloacina, patron divinity of the sewer, and was located near the Tabernae novae in the Forum Romanum. The foundation of the sacellum is extant while images of its likely elevation are found on Roman coins.

name: Temple of Athena Makistos
description: An Athena temple located to the west of Nea Skilloundia, possibly at ancient Makiston, in the Peloponnese.

name: Troulli tis Korfis
description: A hilltop sanctuary, in use from the Bronze Age until the time of the Roman Empire.

name: Esagila
description: The temple of the god Marduk at Babylon. The Sumerian ceremonial name Esagil(a) means "House, Whose Top is High."

name: Ag. Demetrios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Ag. Demetrios

name: Amphiareion
description: The Amphiareion of Oropos was an ancient sanctuary dedicated in the fifth century BCE to the hero Amphiaraos and located in the hills southeast of the port of Oropos.

name: Anthela
description: The Amphictyonic council met at the Shrine of Demeter at Anthela.

name: Aphrodite Phystiou, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B3 Aphrodite Phystiou, T.

name: Temple of Apollo at Tempe
description: The remains of an ancient temple with inscriptions naming Apollo Pythios were revealed by infrastructure works in 1957. These remains were located in the Tempe gorge, opposite the church of Agia Paraskevi.

name: Apollo, T.
description: The oracular shrine of Apollo Koropaios.

name: Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 E4 Apollo, T.

name: Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 F3 Apollo, T.

name: Apollo, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 G3 Apollo, T.

name: Apollo Nasiotas, T.
description: A sanctuary dedicated to Apollo Nasiotas located on the islet of Apsiphia.

name: Apollo Tegyraios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 E3 Apollo Tegyraios, T.

name: Apollo Thourios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Apollo Thourios, T.

name: Archegetes, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Archegetes, T.

name: Artemis Diktynnaia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Artemis Diktynnaia, T.

name: Artemis Elaphebolia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Artemis Elaphebolia, T.

name: Artemis Hegemone, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B3 Artemis Hegemone, T.

name: Artemision
description: The possible site of the temple of Artemis Proseoa beneath the Ag. Georgios chapel, Artemision (Kourbatsi) Euboea.

name: Asklepios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B4 Asklepios, T.

name: Sanctuary of Athena Itonia
description: A pan-thessaliote sanctuary, located at modern Philia Karditsis in Greece.

name: Athena Itonia?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Athena Itonia?, T.

name: Athena Kranaia (temple)
description: The temple of Athena Kranaia, mentioned by Pausanias, was located near ancient Elateia in Phokis.

name: Athena, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Athena, T.

name: Delphi
description: The ancient panhellenic sanctuary of Delphi in Greece, seat of the oracle of Apollo.

name: Demeter, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Demeter, T.

name: Dionysos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Dionysos, T.

name: Iton?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D2 Iton?

name: Kabeirion
description: A sanctuary near Thebes where the mystery cult of the god Kabeiros and his son, Pais, was celebrated.

name: Kenaion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Kenaion

name: Khondro Teikhos
description: A Classical-period sanctuary at Khondro Teikhos, "said to be somewhere NE of Glossa in Skopelos, Sporades, Magnesia, Thessaly" (according to ToposText).

name: Khrysovitsa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B3 Khrysovitsa

name: Korykion Antron (Parnassos)
description: A sacred cave on mount Parnassos named for Apollo's daughter Corycia. It is not to be confused with another cave of the same name at Corycus in Cilicia.

name: Krounoi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B4 Krounoi

name: Magoula Kastri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C1 Magoula Kastri

name: Markesi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 G3 Markesi

name: Mavrovouni
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 E4 Mavrovouni

name: Sanctuary of the Muses
description: The Sanctuary of the Helikonian Muses in Boeotia is celebrated by Hesiod. Archaeological remains of part of the sanctuary in the valley of the Muses date to the third and second centuries B.C.

name: Phokikon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Phokikon

name: Sanctuary of Ptoan Apollo
description: Described by Pausanias, this sanctuary was located near Akraiphiai in Boeotia.

name: Pyra
description: Pyra is a place with preserved ruins of a Doric temple dating to the third century B.C. The sources connect Pyra with the pyre of the hero Herakles.

name: Serapis, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Serapis, T.

name: Taxiarkhis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B3 Taxiarkhis

name: Thetideion
description: A sanctuary of Thetis and/or settlement in the ancient territory of Pharsalus. Scholars have suggested various locations for the ancient site, including Ag. Athanassion at Kato Dasolophos (formerly Bekides) and the area of modern Thetidion (formerly Alchami).

name: Velousia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 G4 Velousia

name: Zeus Akraios, T.
description: A sanctuary of Zeus Akraios on Mount Pelion.

name: Zeus Hypatos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 E4 Zeus Hypatos, T.

name: Antron Kybeles/Mithra
description: A sanctuary of the goddess Meter and her companion Attis, located at modern Kapıkaya in Turkey, 10km northwest of Pergamon. The earliest finds date to the third century BCE. Archaeological finds indicate that Mithras became the principal deity worshipped here in Roman times, down to at least the 4th century CE.

name: Apollo Axyros, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 H4 Apollo Axyros, T.

name: Apollo Karios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F5 Apollo Karios, T.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Anaiitis in Lydia
description: Pausanias describes the Sanctuary of Artemis Anaiitis in Lydia in his third book. Central to his discussion is the xoanon of Artemis that may have been present in the sanctuary, one which he associates with the object Orestes has carried off from Taurus, an episode described by Euripides in his play, Iphigenia in Tauris.

name: Artemis Thermia, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Artemis Thermia, T.

name: Asklepieion
description: The Asklepieion at Pergamon is located 3 km south of the Acropolis.

name: Dionysos Bresagenes, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Dionysos Bresagenes, T.

name: Ai Lia temples
description: An ancient Greek sanctuary located at ancient Keryneia in Achaia.

name: Temple of Horus at Edfu
description: Monumental Ptolemaic temple dedicated to Horus, constructed between 237 BCE and 57 BCE at the settlement of Apollinopolis Magna.

name: Agios Elias Doric temple
description: The Doric temple at Agios Elias, above Asea in Arcadia, dates to ca. 500 B.C.

name: Temple of Divus Augustus (Rome)
description: Caius dedicated a temple to divus Augustus in Rome in the autumn of 37 CE. Based on the sources, the temple should be located between the vicus Tuscus and the vicus Iugarius, just behind the Basilica Iulia. The temple remains unexcavated, and its nature and location are a matter of scholarly debate.

name: Büyük Çelebi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 A4 Büyük Çelebi

name: Maison Carrée
description: A well-preserved Roman temple built ca. 16 B.C. and dedicated to Caius and Lucius Caesar.

name: Bidoni
description: A monumental sanctuary (26 x 20 m) dedicated to Iuppiter.

name: Capitolium of Verona
description: Excavations have determined the location of the capitolium of Roman Verona, part of an extensive, terraced complex that underpins medieval and later structures of the city. The capitolium itself stretches from the northern end of the modern Piazza Erbe to the via Emilei, lying beneath the Palazzo Maffei, church of San Benedetto, Palazzo Malaspina and surrounding structures.

name: Mortuary Temple of Merenptah
description: Mortuary temple of the Nineteenth Dynasty in the Theban Necropolis.

name: Temple at Santa Maria in Canale
description: A temple of Tuscan type with cella and lateral alae built of ashlar masonry and dated to the 3rd century BCE, now built into a rural church and farmstead complex. The temple is situated along the Via Amerina between Ameria and Tuder, and marks the boundary between those two Umbrian communities. A fragmentary Umbrian bronze inscription (Vetter 229) of around 300 BCE mentioning a deity characterized as "Iovia" was discovered here in the 18th century CE.

name: Samian Poseidon Sanctuary (Triphylia)
description: A cult place of Samian Poseidon erected during the Archaic period and located near Kleidi/Kato Samikon, Ileia, Greece.

name: Sanctuary of Artemis Soteira (Phigaleia)
description: Pausanias describes a Sanctuary of Artemis Soteira located at Phigaleia, noting that it stood atop a level hilltop and that there was a standing cult image made of stone. A sacred procession of the local people commenced from the sanctuary site.

name: Ezida
description: The temple of the god Marduk as Tutu, later of the god Nabû, at Borsippa. The Sumerian ceremonial name Ezida means "True House."

name: Eniggidrukalamasuma
description: The temple of the god Nabû of the ḫarû at Babylon. The Sumerian ceremonial name Eniggidrukalamasuma means "House which Bestows the Scepter of the Land."

name: Ekišnugal
description: The temple of the moon god Nanna/Suen/Sîn at Ur from the third to first millennium B.C. Over the course of its long history, it was rebuilt (and expanded) numerous times. A few of its more famous builders were Narām-Sîn of Agade, Ur-Nammu of Ur, and Nebuchadnezzar II of Babylon.

name: Eanna
description: The temple of the god Anu and the goddess Ištar at Uruk. The Sumerian ceremonial name Eanna means "House of Heaven."

name: Temple complex (Petinesca)
description: A Gallo-Roman sanctuary with an enclosed precinct that was linked to the oppidum.

name: Shrine of Dionysos
description: A rural shrine sacred to Dionysos located at Peristeria, Salamina in Salamis, Attica. The shrine is in the vicinity of the so-called "Cave of Euripides".

name: Caesareum of Alexandria
description: Hellenistic temple constructed by Cleopatra VII and dedicated to Julius Caesar, and later rededicated by Augustus to himself. In 13 BCE Augustus relocated two obelisks from Heliopolis to the site. The temple was converted to a Christian church in the mid-4th century.

name: Sanctuary of Athena Hygieia
description: A small sanctuary of Athena Hygieia ("of health") on the Acropolis of Athens, consisting of a statue and an altar, next to the Propylaia. The sanctuary may have been established in 430/29 BC in response to the Periclean plague or have older roots on the Acropolis.

props.ancientName: ágalma tês Hugieías Athenâs, agalma tes Hygieias Athenas

name: Pre-Erechtheion
description: A small temple on the Acropolis of Athens that might have existed from the 470s B.C. until replaced by the Erechtheion, perhaps in the 420s B.C. It might have hosted the cult of Athena Polias and/or Erechtheus.

name: Pandroseion
description: An open-air precinct dedicated to Pandrosos, located on the Acropolis of Athens, at the west side of the Erechtheion. It contained the sacred olive tree of Athena and an altar of Zeus Herkeios.

name: Temple of Zeus at Nemea
description: A partially reconstructed Doric temple, dating to the 4th century BCE.

name: Asklepieion of Messene
description: The Asklepieion in Ancient Messene is a sanctuary of Asklepios, the Greek god of healing, constructed in 214 BC. The complex includes civic as well as sacred buildings

name: Temple of Vesta at Tibur
description: The Temple of Vesta in Tivoli dates to the early first century B.C. It was once situated on the acropolis of the ancient city. Its ruins now lie within the grounds of the Villa Gregoriana.

name: Elephanta Caves
